name: Sofia
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional. Distinct from Kemp exemplar (contamination/washing rather than ordering/arranging). Should be labelled 'Rituals'.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Sofia held her hand away from her body until she could wash and wouldn't touch anything.
entries.parent_response: I found a bathroom right away so she could wash.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Sofia changed all her clothes when she got home and asked me to wash them separately.
Sofia washed her hands for several minutes and asked me repeatedly whether she got all the germs off.
entries.parent_response: I reassured her she was clean each time.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Sofia wouldn't eat until she'd washed her hands twice and used sanitizer.
entries.parent_response: I let her wash and gave her the sanitizer.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Sofia threw out the rest of her food and wouldn't use that plate again.
entries.parent_response: I threw it out and gave her a new plate.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Sofia has a long routine - wash hands, don't touch anything, get into bed a certain way - or she feels contaminated and starts over.
entries.parent_response: I wait for her to finish, sometimes it takes 30 minutes.

situations: Contact with perceived contaminants (door handles, public restrooms, sneezes, pets, shared food/utensils)
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct. Tests whether the model selects the 'Rituals' label for OCD-style presentations.
